justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Three distinct integration patterns identified and designed correctly — SAP batch/SFTP/XML with 15-min cadence, DHL REST/webhook bidirectional, Analytics Kafka streaming. (2) DHL 500ms webhook constraint explicitly addressed via async ingestion queue with immediate 200 OK response. (3) DHL webhook idempotency addressed via idempotency keys derived from DHL's native event IDs with deduplication at consumer level. (4) SAP silent failure risk identified — SapExportBatch tracks export status, failed batches trigger alerts and retries. (5) Dedicated integration layer recommended via Anti-Corruption Layers per integration, explicitly keeping integration logic out of domain services. (6) Flexibility points clearly state new integrations only need a new adapter on the event bus with zero changes to core.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough design with all three integration patterns not just identified but fully architected with concrete components, queues, and data flows
DHL webhook handling is exemplary — dedicated ingestion queue, sub-500ms ack, idempotency via carrier event IDs, dead-letter queue for poison messages
SAP silent failure risk addressed with SapExportBatch aggregate tracking export status, alerting on consecutive failures, and manual re-export capability
Strong flexibility points — carrier swap (DHL to FedEx), SAP replacement, new integrations all isolated to adapter changes only
Goes beyond requirements with Transactional Outbox pattern recommendation, circuit breaker for DHL API, and out-of-order webhook handling guidance
Implementation guidance provides concrete incremental rollout plan starting with simplest integration (Analytics) and ending with most complex (DHL)

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration. The design isolates each external system behind adapter boundaries, routes all domain events through an internal event bus, and uses outbound projectors to transform and deliver data in each system's required protocol and format.
